justification: All four agents' contributions are represented. Pippen's fail-open behavior and both metrics are included. Dynamic Retry-After computation is explicitly stated. Both ACs are present with correct values. However, Kobe's sorted set recommendation is conflated with MJ's architecture decision — the brief presents sorted sets as THE chosen approach and states MJ's original sliding window counter design was 'corrected,' effectively overriding MJ's decision rather than preserving it as the chosen approach with Kobe's recommendation noted separately. Review notes are not distinct from architecture guidance; Kobe's concerns are woven into the architecture decisions and contradictions_resolved rather than preserved in a separate review_notes section.

observations.text: All four agents (MJ, Bird, Kobe, Pippen) are clearly referenced with their specific contributions attributed
Domain rules are comprehensive: 100 req/60s, 429 with Retry-After, fail-open, rolling window distinction
Pippen's fail-open behavior is thoroughly captured with testable assertions and acceptance criteria
Dynamic Retry-After computation is explicitly detailed with the sorted set oldest-entry calculation
Both key ACs present: 101st request rejected (AC1) and window reset as oldest requests age out (AC2)
Additional boundary-burst AC added, showing deep understanding of the problem
Two required metrics (rate_limit_exceeded_total, redis_unavailable_total) both present

Kobe's sorted set recommendation is presented as the adopted architecture, not as a recommendation alongside MJ's chosen approach. The brief says 'Adopted Kobe's recommendation' and frames MJ's sliding window counter as flawed and replaced, violating the requirement to preserve the distinction between MJ's decision and Kobe's suggestion
No distinct review_notes section — Kobe's concerns about boundary bursts are embedded in architecture_guidance and contradictions_resolved rather than kept separate as review notes

The task_context itself states 'Kobe identified that MJ's original fixed-window design doesn't meet Bird's rolling-window requirement — use Redis sorted sets instead' which frames Kobe as having overruled MJ rather than offered a recommendation
